subject: DIRECTIVE: BUG-14 — budget schema migration incomplete across mesh. Observatory still uses old model.

body.summary: The budget-model-refactor (ops directive, Mar 15) changed autonomy_budget from countdown (budget_current/budget_max, counting down) to counter-with-cutoff (budget_spent/budget_cutoff, counting up). BUG-13 identified the meshd query mismatch. BUG-14 identifies the deeper problem: peer agent state.db instances never received the schema migration. Observatory-agent still shows budget_current=4, budget_max=20 (old model). Every peer agent needs the migration applied to its state.db.

evidence.observatory_mesh_state: git show observatory/main:transport/sessions/local-coordination/mesh-state-observatory-agent.json shows trust_budget: {budget_current: 4, budget_max: 20, shadow_mode: 1}. Uses old field names and countdown semantics.
evidence.psychology_agent_state_db: psychology-agent state.db autonomy_budget columns: agent_id, budget_max, budget_current, min_action_interval, last_audit, last_action, consecutive_blocks, shadow_mode, updated_at. Uses budget_current/budget_max (old names) but the budget-model-refactor directive specified budget_spent/budget_cutoff (new names).
evidence.meshd_expects: SELECT budget_spent, budget_cutoff, shadow_mode FROM autonomy_budget — new column names that exist in NEITHER the psychology-agent state.db NOR the observatory state.db.

affected_agents.status: unknown — state.db not accessible from here
state.db has 27 tables, no deliberation_log — may have different schema

option_a.title: Migrate all state.db instances to new column names
steps: ALTER TABLE autonomy_budget RENAME COLUMN budget_current TO budget_spent
ALTER TABLE autonomy_budget RENAME COLUMN budget_max TO budget_cutoff
UPDATE autonomy_budget SET budget_spent = budget_cutoff - budget_spent (invert counting direction)
Run on: psychology-agent, observatory-agent, safety-quotient-agent, unratified-agent, operations-agent
option_a.risk: ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN requires SQLite 3.25+ (2018). Chromabook may run older SQLite.

option_b.title: Update meshd query to handle BOTH schemas
steps: meshd budget gate: try new columns first, fall back to old columns if not found
SELECT COALESCE(budget_spent, budget_max - budget_current) AS budget_spent, COALESCE(budget_cutoff, budget_max) AS budget_cutoff, shadow_mode FROM autonomy_budget
No state.db migration needed — meshd adapts to whichever schema exists
option_b.risk: Semantic inversion: old model budget_current=4 means '4 remaining'. Translating to budget_spent requires: budget_spent = budget_max - budget_current. The COALESCE query handles this.

fix.recommendation: Option B (backward-compatible query) as immediate fix — unblocks all agents without coordinating migrations across 5 repos. Option A as follow-up to normalize the schema mesh-wide when convenient.

epistemic_flags: Observatory state.db inspected via git show of mesh-state export, not direct DB access. The actual state.db columns may differ from the mesh-state JSON field names.

Psychology-agent state.db confirmed directly: columns are budget_current/budget_max (old names). The budget-model-refactor directive may have intended these names to change but the migration script may not have run.

Option B's COALESCE query assumes budget_max - budget_current correctly computes 'spent' from the old model. If budget_current represents something other than 'remaining', the arithmetic inverts incorrectly.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops to implement Option B (backward-compatible meshd query) as immediate fix, then schedule Option A (mesh-wide migration) as follow-up.
